text: Patient reports gums bleeding when brushing for several months, worse along lower front teeth
transcript_quote: my gums bleed when I brush. It's been going on for months. I spit and it's pink, sometimes more than pink

text: Bleeding occurs most times brushing even with gentle technique
transcript_quote: First I figured I was brushing too hard, army style, but it happens even when I go easy.

text: Patient reports halitosis (bad breath) recently
transcript_quote: my buddy told me — very helpfully, in front of people — that my breath's been rough lately

text: Patient reports cold sensitivity, especially ice water on the right side
transcript_quote: cold stuff has started to zing me. Ice water especially, right side.

text: Denies toothache, night pain, or pain with chewing
transcript_quote: No, nothing like that. No pain-pain.

text: Denies loose teeth, facial/jaw swelling, and fevers
transcript_quote: No, no swelling, nothing loose. I'd have come in quicker for that.

text: Patient has not had a dental visit in years, due to base moves and a deployment
transcript_quote: Honestly? Years. Between the base moves and a deployment in the middle of it, dental kept falling off the list.

text: 35-year-old active-duty patient, generally healthy, referred for dental readiness (went 'red')
transcript_quote: Thirty-five, active duty, generally healthy

text: Brushes twice daily (fast), flosses infrequently
transcript_quote: I brush every day, morning and night. Fast, maybe. Floss... when I remember

text: Never smoked, never used smokeless tobacco
transcript_quote: Never smoked, never dipped

text: High energy drink consumption
transcript_quote: Energy drinks, though, yeah. A lot of them. It's a fuel-based lifestyle.

text: Exam of gingivae: gums red, puffy generally, bleed on light touch, with supra- and subgingival tartar especially lower front and around molars
transcript_quote: Your gums are red and puffy pretty much everywhere, and they bleed at a light touch... There's a solid layer of tartar along the gumline, and I can feel it below the gums too

text: Diagnosis of gingival disease, confined to gum tissue without bone loss
transcript_quote: It is gum disease — gingival disease is the formal name for it.

text: Bitewing dental X-rays taken; bone levels sound with nothing alarming between teeth
transcript_quote: The bone levels look sound, and there's nothing alarming between the teeth.

text: Removal of supragingival plaque and calculus from all teeth performed
transcript_quote: First the scaling above the gumline — all the buildup you can see.

text: Removal of subgingival plaque and calculus from all teeth performed under local anesthesia
transcript_quote: Then below the gumline, around every tooth, which is the part that actually turns this around. I'll numb the areas that need it

text: Gingivectomy/gingivoplasty performed on lower right where gum overgrew and pocketed around a tooth
transcript_quote: I'll do a small gum procedure right there — a gingivectomy, basically trimming and reshaping that little section

text: Application of desensitizing medicament to cold-sensitive spots
transcript_quote: I'll paint a desensitizing medicament onto those zingy spots. It seals up the little channels that make cold feel electric.

text: Fluoride treatment applied to strengthen enamel
transcript_quote: we finish the whole visit with a fluoride treatment to toughen the enamel back up

text: Dental consultation and report completed
transcript_quote: I got the referral note that sent you our way.

text: Post-op instruction: wait ~30 minutes after fluoride to eat, keep food soft today, avoid hot/sharp foods
transcript_quote: Give it about thirty minutes after the fluoride, then keep it soft today... Nothing scalding hot, nothing sharp like chips or crusty bread.

text: Instructed to start gentle salt-water rinses tomorrow for lower right
transcript_quote: Gentle salt-water rinses starting tomorrow for the lower right.

text: Counseled to continue brushing with soft brush and easy hand despite bleeding while healing
transcript_quote: don't stop brushing just because things bleed a little while they heal — soft brush, easy hand

text: Oral health education: soft-bristle brush twice daily for two minutes, bristles angled into gumline with small circles, floss once daily
transcript_quote: Soft-bristle brush, twice a day, two full minutes — angle the bristles into the gumline, little circles... And floss once a day, every day

text: Counseled to reduce energy drink sipping — drink quickly rather than over hours, water in between
transcript_quote: If you're going to have one, drink it and be done. Don't sip one can across four hours. Water in between.

text: Return precautions: call same day for real swelling, pus, fever, or bleeding that won't stop with pressure
transcript_quote: If you get real swelling, pus, fever, or bleeding that won't quit with pressure, you call the office the same day.

text: Follow-up appointment in a few weeks to check healing and gum response
transcript_quote: I want you back in a few weeks so I can check the healing and see how your gums are responding

text: Cleared for morning workout; advised to take it easy tonight and avoid chewing on the treated side today
transcript_quote: Morning workout is fine — just take it easy tonight and keep the chewing off that side today.

text: Medication requested/prescribed during encounter (label unresolved)
Imaging study available from encounter
